name: Caldas de Reis - Cuntis - return via the Umia River
name_original: Caldas de Reis - Cuntis - vuelta por el Río Umia
region_intro: Road cycling around Caldas De Reis offers diverse terrain shaped by its rivers, thermal springs, and lush greenery. The region features the Umia and Bermaña rivers, providing scenic routes along their banks. While there are no major peaks, the landscape includes varied elevation changes and hills, such as those found near the Umia River. Cyclists can expect a mix of well-maintained roads and historical paths, suitable for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Caldas De Reis The most popular road cycling route is Caldas de Reis - Cuntis - return via the Umia River, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Umia River. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Caldas de Reis – Vilagarcía Marina loop from Estación de Portas, a moderate 27.6 miles (44.5 km) path. This route offers views towards the Vilagarcía Marina and varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Path Through the Vineyards – Azucarera de Portas loop from Baión, a 19.8 miles (31.8 km) trail leading through vineyards and agricultural areas, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Road cycling around Caldas De Reis is defined by river valleys, thermal springs, and historical Roman bridges. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Caldas De Reis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 2,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Caldas De Reis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Caldas de Reis?
A: There are over 130 road cycling routes around Caldas de Reis, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 16 easy routes, 80 moderate routes, and 36 challenging routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Caldas de Reis?
A: The terrain around Caldas de Reis is diverse, shaped by the Umia and Bermaña rivers. You'll find a mix of well-maintained roads and historical paths, with varied elevation changes and hills, particularly near the Umia River. While there are no major peaks, the landscape offers engaging climbs and descents, providing a varied experience for cyclists.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Caldas de Reis offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14994307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis – Azucarera de Portas loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 20.7 km route with moderate elevation gain, ideal for a more relaxed ride. There are 16 easy routes in total, providing options for those seeking less challenging rides.

Q: What are some scenic natural features or landmarks I can see along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Caldas de Reis often pass by beautiful natural features and historical landmarks. You can encounter the picturesque Umia River and its banks, and some routes might lead you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features Roman bridges, such as the Segade Bridge and Bermaña Bridge, and Romanesque churches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/570232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Mariña de Carracedo Church&lt;/a&gt;, adding cultural interest to your ride.

Q: Are there any loop routes for road cycling in Caldas de Reis?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Caldas de Reis are designed as loops. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974527170/caldas-de-reis-cuntis-return-via-the-umia-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis - Cuntis - return via the Umia River&lt;/a&gt; (28.5 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis – Vilagarcía Marina loop from Estación de Portas&lt;/a&gt; (44.5 km), which offers views towards the marina.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Caldas de Reis?
A: The region's mild climate generally makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery, ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm, but the presence of rivers and thermal springs provides opportunities for cooling off after a ride. It's always advisable to check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there opportunities to relax or rejuvenate after a ride?
A: Absolutely. Caldas de Reis is renowned for its thermal waters, which have healing properties. After a day of cycling, you can relax and rejuvenate at local spas or in outdoor pools along the river, making it a perfect way to unwind.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Caldas de Reis?
A: The road cycling routes in Caldas de Reis are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty along the rivers, and the historical points of interest like Roman bridges and churches that can be found along the way.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through vineyards or agricultural areas?
A: Yes, some routes offer a glimpse into the region's agricultural landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38200606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Through the Vineyards – Azucarera de Portas loop from Baión&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you through vineyards and other agricultural areas for a unique cycling experience.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Caldas de Reis offers 36 difficult routes. These routes typically feature greater distances and more significant elevation gains, providing a demanding yet rewarding cycling experience through the varied landscape.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific viewpoints are not always marked on every route, the region offers panoramic views, particularly from elevated sections. The Umia River valley itself provides many scenic vistas, and rocky monuments like Pedra Filga and Pedra da Grada offer majestic views of the surrounding area.

Q: Is Caldas de Reis part of any long-distance cycling paths?
A: Yes, Caldas de Reis is a significant stop on the Portuguese Way of the Camino de Santiago. This means you can find well-established paths that are popular with both pilgrims and cyclists, offering segments that can be incorporated into longer rides.
tips: Straddling the interior of Galicia and the coast, Cuntis enjoys a very good location in a strategic position between Santiago de Compostela (27 km) and Pontevedra (30 km). Famous for its natural and medicinal hot springs
